programmes.show_description: Paramedics treat a range of cases, including a man with severe chest pains, a 103-year-old who has had a fall, and a woman who may have a bleed on the brain.
A young girl arrives at King's College Hospital seriously injured after being hit by a car and a bus. Plus, a look at other pedestrians who've ended up as patients in A&E.
This episode focuses on men of all ages facing illness and their fears. Arthur, who's 79, has stomach pains but isn't one to complain. Tom, 20, is having trouble breathing.
The campers visit Ghyll House Farm in Ilkley. Sheep shearing and lamb feeding are new experiences for the critics, but will they embrace the campsite's rural charm?
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Mike and Sue host at The Gables Pod Camping in Escomb, County Durham. Their guests are pleased with the pods, but some awkward tension leaves one guest on a sofa and another in a hot tub.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien uncover a haven of memorabilia in Dumfries and Galloway, including an unloved corner unit.
Sue and Brian love the Spanish culture and lifestyle, and want their very own holiday home in the charming town of Axarquía. Can Jasmine Harman find their perfect place for £180,000?
Essex area. The first host, colourful 46-year-old head teacher Corinna, plans a royal-themed evening. (Ep1/5)
Essex area. On the second night, money broker Paul plans an evening of fine dining. And he's travelled more than 300 miles to source his ingredients! (Ep2/5)
Essex area. Teacher Harmy plans to spice up her guests' lives with an authentic Punjabi feast. But is it too hot for them to handle? (Ep3/5)
Essex area. Recruitment consultant Adam, who's 25, hopes to exceed his guests' expectations with a menu of posh nosh... at his dad's house. (Ep4/5)
Essex area. On the final night, 24-year-old Grace, who's a lover of all things Essex, plans to dazzle her guests with a glamorous party. (Ep5/5)
Roman and Tamsyn take a no-nonsense approach to running The White Swan in North Yorkshire. But their B&B doesn't meet Farid and John's high standards, and there are grumbles at breakfast.
Lisa and Sally hope that their generosity of spirit will be a hit with their guests at Clough View Farm in Derbyshire. But there's yet more grumbling after a long wait at breakfast.
The third visit is to The Buxted Inn in Uckfield, East Sussex, run by Nick and chef David. Lisa is charmed by the character, but Farid and John think it has a corporate feel.
The final visit is to the Crescent Victoria hotel in Margate, Kent, where Farid and John hope to dazzle their guests with a stay fit for a queen. Can they get the breakfast right?
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for the last time. After dramatic discoveries, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Ford Mondeo: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw head to Hampshire to fix up a 2000 Ford Mondeo for Karl, a widowed dad of two girls who's been hit by tragedy.
Riley RMF: In Rochdale, Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw fix up a unique British post-war classic: a 1953 Riley RMF that's been handed down from father to son for 70 years.
In Birmingham, Kirstie Allsopp helps indecisive Elle and her fiancé Ed find the perfect pad, while Phil Spencer helps Ben and Laura make the move from Bristol to Birmingham.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Staines-upon-Thames, Kirstie and Phil help a couple decide what to do with their dormer bungalow. Nitasha sees its potential, but Nikunj isn't sure if it's the one.
With unprecedented access to an investigation into an allegation of police corruption, detectives from the Major Crime Unit track down, arrest and then charge a fellow officer.
This episode focuses on patients involved in road traffic accidents. Steve, who's 48, was found unconscious at the wheel of his car. Scarlett, 11, has been knocked down by a car.
The heli-medics help a man with a single back wound who's not sure if he's been shot or stabbed. And a cyclist needs immediate life-saving treatment on the roadside.
The heli-medics race to help Yvonne, who's in cardiac arrest. And in the Peaks, they land on top of a steep ridge where Donna collapsed while out walking.
Paramedics are called to a man involved in a car crash, a 19-year-old who's fallen down the stairs and a photographer who wasn't looking where they were going.
This episode focuses on patients involved in road traffic accidents. Steve, who's 48, was found unconscious at the wheel of his car. Scarlett, 11, has been knocked down by a car.
The final stop in North Yorkshire is The Dalesbridge campsite. The experience is dampened by bad weather, but the stunning scenery and live music keep the critics in good spirits.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
It's the final day and the B&Bers meet one last time to settle some scores and find out how much they've been paid. After some hosting, roasting and dust disputes, the winner's announced.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Pembrokeshire, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet a cattle breeder whose barns are brimming with treasure, and Phil, whose vintage pedal car makes the boys feel young at heart.
Sisters Lynne and Samantha are pooling their money to buy their dream property in the beautiful Spanish countryside of inland Cádiz. Danni Menzies shows them what their £60k can buy.
Edinburgh. The first host, 27-year-old interior designer and gentle giant James, plans to blow his guests' minds with his spicy Mexican menu. (Ep1/5)
Edinburgh. Grandmother Katy plans an out-of-season 'Christmas tartan' evening, complete with costumes, games and a few revelations. (Ep2/5)
Edinburgh. Glamorous solicitor Aisling hosts an Irish party. She has a piper on her doorstep, but Katy's not a fan of her main, and the guests all struggle with 'clean eating'. (Ep3/5)
Edinburgh. American writer Harry serves up a menu inspired by his family, while Bruce breaks his own etiquette rules, and Aisling draws Bruce's portrait with razor-sharp hilarity. (Ep4/5)
Edinburgh. Style-conscious Bruce hosts the final night. All week he's expressed a taste for the finer things in life. With expectations high, can he put his food where his mouth is? (Ep5/5)
The first hosts, Karen MacDonald and Geoff Thomson, hope to offer a slice of Zen at their relaxing retreat, Just B, in Edinburgh. But there are dirty discoveries in a dog-friendly room.
The second visit is to Glencoe Villa Guest House in Featherstone, West Yorkshire, owned by Ewa Hunt. But the homely bedrooms aren't to everyone's taste and there's a debate over dinner.
Uncle and nephew Stefan Carron and Nick Edwards host at The Fox Inn in Stourton, Staffordshire. Will Stefan's celebrity name dropping impress their guests?
Tony Carlin and his partner Natalie Briggs host the final visit, at The New Inn in Derbyshire. But some of the guests question its 'luxury' label, and there's a no-show at breakfast.
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet to find out how much they've been paid. After dramatic discoveries and cutting comments, the winner is announced.
Porsche 911 Targa: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw head to Oxfordshire to renovate a rundown 1973 Porsche 911 Targa for Ben, who's had it for 35 years.
Jeep Grand Cherokee: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw head for Somerset to turn former soldier Ben's run-down classic Jeep into an off-roading machine.
Kirstie's house-hunting in north east London with Nicol and his brother Harry, who are pooling their funds. And Phil helps Tinder success story Hiren and Priyanka find a home.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Phil Spencer revisits Kathleen and Tom, who decided to renovate and stay in their Victorian terrace in Newcastle back in 2016. Their lives are now radically different.
Brand new series - I Dance Alone: When Marinella's son Gabriele goes missing, Blanca hears a detail in a voice message that confirms he's been abducted. In Italian/subs.
A touching episode dedicated to love, from the concerned parents of an unresponsive child to the devoted sisters of a 52-year-old who has Down's syndrome.
